gradingAgent.canvas.palette.title: Add nodes
One output per quiz question (supports shuffled delivery and question banks).
gradingAgent.canvas.palette.codeTestsDis: Enable code execution in platform settings to autograde code submissions.
gradingAgent.canvas.palette.studentGrade: Fixed on the canvas — wire grade sources into this sink.
gradingAgent.canvas.palette.hint: Click or drag a node onto the canvas.
The student's files and text entry.
Run a custom AI prompt on connected inputs.
Grade one rubric criterion with AI.
Branch the workflow when a condition is met.
Combine multiple scores into one grade.
Override the grade with a fixed score value.
Pause for instructor approval before posting.
Send a submission to the review queue.
Final grade sink — already on the canvas.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.title: Inspector
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.empty: Select a node to edit its settings.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.outputHelp: Wire grade and comments sources into the fixed Student Grade output.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.outputGrad: Required. Connect a grade source (Grader, Criterion Grader, AI, Code Test Runner, or Score Aggregator).
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.outputComm: Optional. Connect a comments source to write an instructor comment.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.submission: Read-only source for the open student submission. Wire its output into a Grader node.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.quizRespon: Read-only preview of each delivery slot. Wire question outputs into grading nodes.
Add manually graded questions to this quiz.
This slot may draw from a question bank at attempt time.
Question order may differ for each student.
Question details are unavailable for this slot.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.submission: Choose a student submission in the header to preview files.
This submission has no files or typed text yet.
gradingAgent.failure.emptyFileText: Submission file has no extractable text
gradingAgent.failure.visionNotEnabled: Vision grading is not enabled for image-only submissions
gradingAgent.failure.visionWorkflow: Vision grading is not supported for complex workflow graphs
gradingAgent.failure.visionPageCap: Submission exceeds the vision page limit
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.activityHe: Provides assignment content and rubric outputs. Connect Content and/or Rubric into a Grader node.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.activityAs: Content and rubric outputs come from this assignment.
gradingAgent.canvas.nodes.group.title: Group
gradingAgent.canvas.nodes.group.subtitle: {count, plural, one {# node} other {# nodes}} · double-click to open
gradingAgent.canvas.group.groupSelection: Group {count, plural, one {# node} other {# nodes}}
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.criterionG: Scores one rubric criterion in isolation. Wire the same inputs as a Grader node, then connect grade outputs to a Score Aggregator or directly to Student Grade for single-criterion rubrics.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.criterionU: Using this assignment's rubric. Wire Activity → Rubric for an explicit rubric source.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.criterionW: Wire an Activity rubric output or use an assignment with a rubric to choose a criterion.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.criterionO: The model must return JSON with a single score, rationale, and confidence for the bound criterion.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.aiHelp: Runs a prompt against the wired input. Chain multiple AI nodes or connect from input sources.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.aiCompiled: Resolved prompt and wired input sent to the model after the latest dry run.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.aiOutputFo: Rubric input is wired — the model must return JSON with rubric criterion IDs and scores.
No rubric input is wired — the model must return JSON with a total points score.
This system prompt is fixed by Lextures and cannot be edited.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.codeTestsH: Runs the wired submission against your test cases in the sandbox and outputs a deterministic grade plus a per-test report.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.codeTestsN: Add at least one test case.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.routerHelp: Route submissions or grades down Then or Else based on a deterministic rule — no LLM call.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.flagForRev: Terminal sink that sends the submission to a human review queue instead of writing a grade.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.setScoreHe: Outputs a fixed score regardless of upstream grades. Useful on a Conditional Router branch, e.g. to assign 0 when a submission is blank.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.setScoreVa: The exact score passed to the Student Grade node.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.setScoreCo: e.g. No submission — score set to 0
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.aggregator: Combines multiple grade inputs into one total using sum, weighted average, min/max, or rubric merge.
Wire grade sources into this node to set weights.
Merge upstream comments with source labels
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.reviewGate: Holds low-confidence or flagged grades for human approval before writing to the gradebook.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.originalit: Reads the stored originality report for this submission and exposes similarity, report text, and a flag for routing.
AI-likelihood is disabled by platform policy. Use similarity or enable plagiarism checks in platform settings.
AI-likelihood detectors can misclassify non-native writing. Prefer routing to human review over auto-penalties.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.referenceH: Provides trusted reference text — a model answer, answer key, or source texts — for graders to compare against the submission.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.referenceT: Reference material is sent as trusted context. Do not paste another student's work here.
Paste the model answer, answer key, or excerpted source text…
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.referenceC: {count} characters — will truncate to {max} at run time
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.referenceF: When selected, text is extracted from the file at run time (inline text is ignored).
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.rubricHelp: Provides a standalone rubric output. Use this assignment's rubric, another assignment's rubric, or an inline definition.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.rubricAssi: Uses the rubric configured on this assignment.
This assignment has no rubric. Add one in assignment settings or choose another source.
gradingAgent.canvas.inspector.rubricLibr: Loads the rubric from the selected assignment at run time.
